fallback: No local cangjie skill was available in this runtime. The cangjie search returned no matching skill files, so this entry was distilled using book2skill and skill-creator-style guidance.
metadataNotes: The assigned Microsoft Learn source snapshot is a 404 page for an intended transactional outbox pattern URL, so source content was incomplete. Supplemented the broader theory metadata for Transaction Processing: Concepts and Techniques by Jim Gray and Andreas Reuter, Morgan Kaufmann, 1992, plus operational transaction-processing concepts needed for auto-orchestrator design: ACID boundaries, isolation, recovery, idempotency, transactional outbox/inbox, sagas, leases/fencing, retries, and compensation.

closureReview.scope: Closed-loop review of SKILL.md, test-prompts.json, and audit.json.

qualityChecks.standaloneKnowledge: SKILL.md defines invariants, commit boundaries, consistency, idempotency, outbox/inbox, sagas, recovery, compensation, tests, and boundaries without requiring source files.
qualityChecks.runtimeDependencyPolicy: External files, webpages, original books, source reports, external source folder, distilled source material, and out-of-directory material are provenance only and not runtime dependencies.
qualityChecks.triggerContract: Trigger, non-trigger, activation gate, and execution gate are explicit.
qualityChecks.workflowAndDeliverables: Workflow, executable correctness deliverables, failure/recovery/idempotency, and hard rules are present.
qualityChecks.testPromptCoverage: test-prompts.json includes should_trigger, should_not_trigger, edge_cases, and compliance_expectations.

remainingRisks: Assigned web source was a 404 snapshot, but all runtime transaction-processing concepts needed by the skill are compressed into SKILL.md.

runtimeDependencyPolicy: Closed skill: execute from SKILL.md; use references/source-notes.md only as local optional provenance; do not require external source folders, webpages, original books, source reports, machine-local paths, or cross-skill files.
